Cyber Attack Ensnares $4.3T Muni Market's Key Site
data.description:
Concerns over cyber risks is growing in the municipal market as MuniOS got a ransomware attack that disrupted state and local borrowers' ability to post debt documents on this platform for several days, until it went back online Thursday morning. Bloomberg's reporter Erin Hudson discussed the story on "Bloomberg Markets" with Scarlet Fu.

Bloomberg videos, provided by MT Newswires

data.title: NEP Broadcasting, LLC announced that it has received $700 million in funding from 26North Partners LP, The Carlyle Group Inc.
data.description: NEP Broadcasting, LLC announced that it has received $700 million in round of funding led by new investor 26North Partners LP on October 17, 2025. The transaction included the participation from returning investors Carlyle Global Partners LP, a fund managed by The Carlyle Group Inc. The company issued convertible preferred stock in the transaction.

data.title: Unifor Stellantis council says it rejects decision to relocate production from Brampton to US
Porsche AG Announces CEO Changes
data.description:
The Supervisory Board of Porsche AG has appointed Dr. Michael Leiters as CEO of Porsche AG, effective as of January 1, 2026. Dr. Oliver Blume, who has led Porsche AG as CEO for ten years, will continue to serve as CEO of Volkswagen Group. As CEO of Porsche AG, Oliver Blume paved the way for record financial years, the IPO of Porsche AG, and the expansion into further international markets, as well as historic motorsport successes.

He prepared the generational change in the Porsche Executive Board in a long-term and strategic manner. Dr. Oliver Blume's successor will be Dr. Michael Leiters. Leiters has been CEO of McLaren Automotive since July 2022, having previously served as CTO for Ferrari for more than eight years.

Leiters is no stranger to Porsche AG. Before taking up his position at Ferrari, he held positions at Porsche for more than 13 years, most recently with responsibility for the Porsche Macan and Porsche Cayenne series.

New Yorkers Face Likely Tax Hikes No Matter Who Becomes Mayor
data.description:

Revolution Medicines Rises as FDA Grants Priority Designation for Cancer Treatment
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan


Shares of Revolution Medicines gained after the Food and Drug Administration granted a priority voucher to speed up the development and review of the company's cancer treatment.

The stock rose 8.9% to $54.09 on Friday. Shares are up 24% this year.

The clinical oncology company said its daraxonrasib inhibitor received one of the first vouchers under the Commissioner's National Priority Voucher pilot program.

The program, announced in June, aims to accelerate the development and review of certain drugs that are aligned with U.S. national health priorities.

Daraxonrasib is being studied in two Phase 3 trials in patients with previously treated metastatic pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma and with previously treated metastatic non-small cell lung cancer.


Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-17-25 1413ET

Nestlé S.A. Plans to Slash 16,000 Jobs
data.description:
Nestlé S.A.'s new chief executive officer announced plans to slash 16,000 jobs only weeks after taking over, aiming to build on a stronger-than-expected increase in quarterly sales that lifted its shares by the most in 17 years. The job reductions, amounting to about 6% of the workforce, will occur over the next two years, the maker of Nespresso coffee capsules and KitKat candy bars said on October 16, 2025. Of the planned job cuts, some 12,000 will be among white-collar staff, with the rest from manufacturing and supply chain roles.

Any job losses through divestments won't be counted toward the 16,000 planned reductions.

data.title: More ECB rate cuts increasingly unlikely, Wunsch says
data.description:
WASHINGTON (Reuters) -The chances of further interest rate cuts from the European Central Bank are declining given the economy's resilience and inflation holding around the 2% target, Belgian central bank Governor Pierre Wunsch said on Friday.

The ECB cut rates by a combined 2 percentage points in the year to June but has been on hold ever since as policymakers contemplate if further easing may be needed due to the potential for consumer prices to rise too slowly.

"Given that the economy is proving resilient, the chance that further rate cuts will be needed is receding," Wunsch told Reuters on the sidelines of the IMF's annual meeting. "I'm quite comfortable where we are today, though I remain open."

Still, risks are skewed towards interest rates going lower, since the impact of tariffs is yet to fully play out, the dollar is weak and there is the risk of China dumping surplus goods, Wunsch added.

Inflation is set to dip to 1.7% next year before rising back to target in subsequent years, fuelling concern among some policymakers, particularly around the bloc's southern edge, that price growth could get stuck below target.

"I don't see any major risks for inflation either on the upside or downside," Wunsch said. "But I also don't get nervous about numbers like 1.8% or 2.2%," he added.

Wunsch also said that not all deviations from the target required the same policy response and the ECB needed to study the underlying fundamentals in determining the intensity of its policy action.

"If we're growing at potential, the labour market is healthy, but inflation is still below target, I would support a mildly supportive stance but definitely not aggressive accommodation," he said.

Wunsch also said he was comfortable with market pricing for interest rates, which sees close to no chance of a rate cut this year and see a one in two probability of a move by next June.

(Reporting by Balazs KoranyiEditing by Peter Graff)
